plan.title: Integrate gate clearances and capacity blocks into Gate 0 and swarm:launch

narratives.Description: Threads judgment-gate clearances and capacity block modes into the story-start Gate 0 and the headless swarm:launch consent token, and appends authority-bearing events to the durable audit log. Block-gated stories ship solo in v1 until per-commit trailer attribution lands.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1. Extend scripts/preflight_story_start.py and scripts/swarm_launch.py to read gate_clearances from the allocation context and fail closed on an uncleared active block gate. 2. Add tests in tests/cli/test_preflight_story_start.py and tests/cli/test_swarm_launch.py asserting an uncleared block gate aborts dispatch and a recorded clearance permits it.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm operator, I want gated stories blocked at dispatch until cleared, so that sensitive work cannot launch without human sign-off.

items.title: Given an active block gate without clearance, when verify:story-ready runs, then it aborts dispatch with a non-zero exit.

narrative.Acceptance: Given an active block gate without clearance, when verify:story-ready runs, then it aborts dispatch with a non-zero exit.

items.title: Given a recorded gate clearance in the allocation context, when swarm:launch runs, then it permits the gated story to launch.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a recorded gate clearance in the allocation context, when swarm:launch runs, then it permits the gated story to launch.

items.title: When an allocation approval is granted, then the integration appends an authority-bearing entry to the durable audit log.

narrative.Acceptance: When an allocation approval is granted, then the integration appends an authority-bearing entry to the durable audit log.

expected_outputs: Gate 0 and swarm:launch fail closed on an uncleared active block gate
authority-bearing clearance events append to the durable audit log

references.title: Issue #1419: RFC: Agentic Prioritization & Coordination Model for Deft Directive
